title: Space Systems Operations
description: Operates satellite tracking, ballistic missile detection, and space launch support systems. Monitors low-orbit and deep-space satellites, identifies missile launches, and performs on-orbit satellite command operations for GPS and military communications satellites.

title: All Source Intelligence Analyst
description: Oversees intelligence-gathering initiatives and manages, develops, and evaluates intelligence information collected from multiple sources. Produces assessments to support commanders' mission decisions.

title: Geospatial Intelligence Analyst
description: Analyzes satellite and aerial imagery to identify unusual activity and potential threats. Uses mapping tools for target location and distance calculations, creates intelligence briefings, and supports targeting operations.

title: Signals Intelligence Analyst
description: Extracts and analyzes foreign communications through electromagnetic emissions. Operates intelligence equipment, manipulates signals data, and produces intelligence reports to help commanders respond to threats.

title: Fusion Analyst
description: Performs all-source intelligence analysis by fusing data across multiple disciplines. Validates target development, manages target priorities, supports mission planning, and assesses potential threats across multiple domains.

title: Cyber Warfare Operations
description: Conducts offensive and defensive cyberspace operations. Plans, executes, and assesses cyberspace operations to achieve military objectives in and through the cyberspace domain.

title: Cyber Defense Operations
description: Protects Space Force networks and information systems from cyber threats. Performs network security monitoring, incident response, vulnerability assessment, and defensive cyberspace operations.

title: Cyber Defense Operations (Radio Frequency/Satellite)
description: Specializes in defending satellite communications and radio frequency systems. Monitors and secures RF and SATCOM infrastructure against cyber intrusions and electronic threats.

title: Cyber Systems Operations
description: Operates and maintains cyberspace systems and networks. Installs, configures, and troubleshoots network infrastructure, servers, and communication systems supporting Space Force operations.

title: Cyber Transport Systems
description: Installs, operates, and maintains ground-based radio frequency, satellite, and network transport systems. Manages switching, routing, and long-haul communications infrastructure.

title: Computer Systems Programming
description: Designs, codes, tests, and maintains software applications and computer systems. Develops programs to support Space Force command and control and operational systems.

title: Electrical Systems
description: Installs, maintains, and repairs electrical power distribution systems, interior wiring, and associated equipment at Space Force installations.

title: Electrical Power Production
description: Operates, maintains, and repairs electric power generation equipment including generators and uninterruptible power supplies supporting Space Force facilities and mission systems.

title: Pavement and Construction Equipment
description: Operates heavy construction equipment and performs pavement maintenance, including runways and roads at Space Force installations.

title: Structural
description: Constructs, maintains, and repairs buildings, structures, and facilities at Space Force installations. Performs carpentry, masonry, and structural metalwork.

title: Water and Fuel Systems Maintenance
description: Installs, operates, and maintains water distribution, wastewater, and liquid fuels systems at Space Force installations.

title: Engineering
description: Plans, designs, and manages construction and engineering projects. Surveys land, prepares design drawings, and supervises civil engineering work at Space Force installations.

title: Personnel
description: Manages personnel programs including military assignments, promotions, separations, and human resources functions for Space Force Guardians.

title: Services
description: Manages force support programs including lodging, food service, recreation, and mortuary affairs. Provides quality-of-life services to Space Force Guardians and families.

title: Safety
description: Develops and implements safety programs to prevent accidents and occupational illnesses. Investigates mishaps, conducts safety inspections, and advises commanders on safety compliance at Space Force installations.

title: Contracting
description: Manages the acquisition of supplies, services, and construction through federal contracting processes. Negotiates contracts, ensures regulatory compliance, and manages vendor relationships for Space Force programs.

title: Financial Management and Comptroller
description: Manages Space Force budgets, financial operations, and accounting functions. Processes pay and allowances, maintains financial records, and provides fiscal guidance to commanders.

title: Special Investigations
description: Conducts criminal investigations, counterintelligence operations, and fraud inquiries. This is not an entry-level position; Guardians must serve in another career field first before applying.

title: Materiel Management
description: Manages supply chain operations including inventory control, equipment accountability, and logistics support. Ensures Space Force units have the equipment and supplies needed for mission success.

title: Air Transportation
description: Plans and coordinates the movement of personnel, cargo, and equipment by air. Manages aerial port operations and ensures proper loading, documentation, and delivery of Space Force assets.

title: Recruiter
description: Represents the Space Force by identifying and attracting qualified individuals for enlistment. Conducts outreach, interviews applicants, and guides candidates through the enlistment process.
